picks.title: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
picks.why: Kids get hands-on with porcelain casting from a renowned Hungarian designer—messy, creative, unforgettable.

picks.title: Asian & Pacific Islander Heritage Month Kick Off at King Library
picks.why: Launch API Heritage Month with art installations and celebration—perfect cultural immersion for curious spring breakers.

picks.title: Road Scholar: Easter Island at Los Altos Library
picks.why: Road Scholar presenter brings Easter Island's mysterious Moai statues to life with fascinating stories and photos.

picks.title: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
picks.why: Wildlife photographer Joan Sparks shows stunning images of elegant wading birds—educational and visually captivating.

picks.title: Craft Club for Adults: The Joy of Drawing - Tulips in Spring
picks.why: Paint tulips in spring—adults join kids for joyful watercolor workshop celebrating the season together.

picks.title: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
picks.why: SJSU Opera's quirky double bill, especially 'Stuck Elevator,' offers hilarious, unexpected performance art for families.

picks.title: Watercolor Workshop for Adults
picks.why: Free watercolor workshop where all supplies provided—no registration needed, perfect spontaneous spring break art outing.

picks.title: Board Game Sunday!
picks.why: Monthly board game gathering at Los Altos Library—casual, social, and free for all skill levels.

picks.title: San Francisco Shakespeare Festival Presents Julius Caesar
picks.why: Shakespeare's *Julius Caesar* comes alive outdoors in Cupertino—classic drama performed by professional festival actors.

picks.title: Stylish Sustainability Craft
picks.why: Upcycle old clothes into fashion art for Earth Day—hands-on sustainability craft with local artist guidance.

picks.title: Archive Room: Ester Hernandez | Selections from Special Collections at Stanford Libraries
picks.why: Stanford's Cantor Arts Center showcases Chicana printmaker Ester Hernandez—accessible, powerful art history for all ages.

picks.title: Archive Room: Ruth Asawa | Selections from Special Collections at Stanford Libraries
picks.why: Ruth Asawa's innovative wire sculptures and art advocacy story captivate—museum quality inspiration for young artists.
